pi_items.source: Verified across all task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) and environment setup (setup_freecad.sh). Task description specifies ~/Documents/FreeCAD/enclosure.FCStd which expands to /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/enclosure.FCStd.
Task description explicitly specifies: rectangle with outer dimensions 100 mm x 60 mm, height 25 mm via Pad operation.

Calculated from specifications: outer volume 150,000 mm³ - inner cavity (96×56×23 mm) 123,648 mm³ = 26,352 mm³ shell volume. This falls within the claimed range. Verified through FreeCAD Shell/Thickness operation documentation.

Tolerance of ±2mm is reasonable for mechanical design (±2% of 100mm dimension, ±3.3% of 60mm, ±8% of 25mm). Consistent with typical CAD manufacturing tolerances. Used in verifier.py for dimension validation.

privileged_info_summary: The task specifies creation of a hollow rectangular enclosure with verified dimensions of 100×60×25 mm outer size and 2 mm uniform wall thickness via FreeCAD's Part Design workbench (Sketch → Pad → Thickness/Shell operation with top face removed). The expected output file path is /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/enclosure.FCStd. The calculated shell volume of 26,352 mm³ falls within the specified range [20,000-35,000] mm³. A dimensional tolerance of ±2.0 mm is applied during verification.
